title: Accessibility Features for Forms Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential techniques and attributes for making your web forms inclusive and accessible.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of the <label> tag in an HTML form?
options: To make the field load faster.
To provide a clickable label and a screen-readable description for each input.
To define the page's structure and layout.
To hide images from keyboard users.

questions.question: Which of these is the correct way to group related inputs (like an address) to provide context for screen readers?
What happens when a user focuses on an input that has an 'aria-describedby' attribute pointing to an error message?
options: The screen reader will read the input's label AND the error message.
The browser ignores the error message.

questions.question: Which attribute tells assistive technologies that a specific field MUST be filled out?
Keyboard users rely on the ________ to know which form field they are currently interacting with.
